title: Implement the MCP server (thin wrappers over core)
description: Expose the core ledger over MCP stdio (spec §14) so coding agents can drive Waystation directly. Tools are thin wrappers over existing core functions — no new behavior. A `waystation mcp` command starts the stdio server.
acceptance: Tools: get_status, get_next_task, get_task, claim_task, release_task, get_brief, render_prompt, create_handoff, list_issues, create_issue, post_message, get_inbox, validate_ledger.
Each tool calls the same core logic as the CLI and returns structured results (reusing the CommandResult/Diagnostic shape where sensible).
Writes go through the single core write path (lock + atomic write + events).
bun:test covers a client calling get_next_task and claim_task over an in-memory transport.
notes: Phase 4 (roadmap). Depends on task-mcp-smoke confirming the SDK on Bun. Do not reimplement writes — reuse src/core.
